channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How global military spending and warfare contribute to rising greenhouse gas emissions and environmental damage
item.description: The article examines the environmental and climate impacts of global militarism, arguing that modern warfare and military spending significantly contribute to greenhouse gas emissions and long-term ec...

item.title: PSG and Arsenal Ready for Champions League Final in Budapest
item.description: Arsenal are set to face the defending champions, Paris Saint-Germain, in the 2026 Champions League final this Saturday at the Puskas Arena in Budapest, Hungary. The match is scheduled to kick off at 7...

item.title: Rafael Jodar denies contact with ball girl after incident during French Open match
item.description: Spanish tennis player Rafael Jodar has denied any wrongdoing after a brief on-court incident during his third-round match at the French Open sparked debate on social media. Footage circulating online ...

item.title: Manyara Regional Commissioner directs expansion of water, sanitation and hygiene services across the region
item.description: The Regional Commissioner for Manyara Region has issued strong directives calling for comprehensive improvements in Water, Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH) services, with a clear emphasis on ensuring tha...

item.title: Study Finds Beetroot Juice Can Lower Blood Pressure in Older Adults by Supporting Oral Microbiome
item.description: Recent research has revealed that drinking nitrate-rich beetroot juice twice daily for two weeks can reduce blood pressure in older adults by positively influencing their oral microbiome. The study, p...

item.title: Nigerian Army arrests suspect linked to recruitment fraud in Nigeria
item.description: The Nigerian Army has arrested a suspect connected to recruitment fraud, according to Channels Television. The development highlights ongoing concerns about dishonest practices in military recruitment...

item.title: Calls for greater transparency over Donald Trump’s health and fitness for office
item.description: The article is a critical opinion piece arguing that Donald Trump should be more transparent about his physical and mental health, amid growing public concern about his fitness for the presidency. It ...

item.title: Kakamega Primary Teacher Shows Off Backflip During Outdoor Lesson
item.description: O’kubasu Malik, a primary school teacher from Kakamega County, recently gave his pupils a memorable experience by performing a backflip during an outdoor session. The event happened while the children...

item.title: Free Dog Vaccination Campaign Launched Across Arusha to Eliminate Rabies
item.description: Mbwa Wa Africa Animal Rescue teamed up with Arusha City Council, Ministry of Livestock and Fisheries, and Edgard & Cooper Foundation to start a free four-day mass dog vaccination campaign in Arusha Ci...

item.title: Federal Judge Rules Trump’s Name Must Be Removed from Kennedy Center; Renovation Plans Partially Blocked
item.description: A federal judge has ruled that the Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts in Washington, D.C., must remove President Donald Trump’s name from its signage and official designation, finding that the ins...

item.title: U.S. and Iran agree to 60-day ceasefire extension pending presidential approval
item.description: The United States and Iran have reportedly reached an agreement to extend the ceasefire for another 60 days while negotiators tackle key issues, including Iran’s nuclear program and regional security ...

item.title: Pembroke House School supports Utumishi Girls fire victims with free food and relief aid
item.description: A wave of compassion has been witnessed in Nakuru County after a devastating fire at Utumishi Girls Academy, which claimed 16 lives and left several students injured. In the aftermath of the tragedy, ...

item.title: AAP secures clear majority in Punjab civic polls; Congress second, BJP trails in fifth position
item.description: The Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) has registered a decisive victory in the Punjab civic elections, emerging as the dominant political force across municipal wards. According to the Punjab State Election Commi...

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Jordan trial time limits while clarifying exceptions for delays
item.description: The Supreme Court of Canada has reaffirmed the validity of the Jordan framework, which sets strict time limits for criminal trials under Section 11(b) of the Charter of Rights and Freedoms, while also...

item.title: Trial hears ex-footballer denies direct role in stabbing of Iranian journalist, blaming alleged accomplice
item.description: A former professional Romanian footballer has told a London court that he was not the person who stabbed an Iranian journalist in a suspected politically motivated attack. Nandito Badea, 21, who previ...

item.title: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani Defends Sanctuary City Status Against Homeland Security Threats
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani strongly responded to comments made by Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin regarding potential penalties for sanctuary cities. Mullin had stated that his de...

item.title: Tanzania and WaterAid UK strengthen partnership to improve water and sanitation services
item.description: Tanzania and WaterAid UK have pledged to deepen their cooperation to improve water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) services across the country. This agreement was reached during the African Developmen...

item.title: Canada Post posts $205 million quarterly loss amid labour uncertainty and restructuring efforts
item.description: Canada Post has reported a pre-tax loss of $205 million for the first quarter of 2026, reflecting ongoing financial difficulties as the Crown corporation continues to face declining mail and parcel vo...

item.title: Themba Zwane’s leadership tested in Bafana Bafana’s final World Cup friendly
item.description: As Bafana Bafana gear up for the Fifa World Cup next month, their final warm-up match at Orlando Stadium ended in a goalless draw against Nicaragua, leaving supporters slightly disappointed. The match...

item.title: Malacañang declares special non-working holidays in six Philippine areas for June commemorations
item.description: Malacañang has announced the declaration of special non-working holidays in six areas across the Philippines in June to allow residents to participate in local commemorations and festivities. Accordin...
